section_title: If the pawnor makes default in payment of the debt, or performance, at
content:
176. If the pawnor makes default in payment of the debt, or performance, at
right where
the stipulated time of the promise, in respect of which the goods were
pawnor
makes pledged, the pawnee may bring a suit against the pawnor upon the debt or
default
promise, and retain the goods pledged as a collateral security; or he may sell
the thing pledged, on giving the pawnor reasonable notice of the sale.
If the proceeds of such sale are less than the amount due in respect of the
debt or promise, the pawnor is still liable to pay the balance. If the proceeds
of the sale are greater than the amount so due, the pawnee shall pay over
the surplus to the pawnor.
